spec_match.rationale: Exercises the three documented spec values for anchor-scope: `none`, `all`, and `<dashed-ident>` (single name). Toggle drives a class swap that flips the value.

interactive.rationale: Real <select> driving real CSS class changes against two parallel widgets — the floaters move when scope changes.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card promises a toggle between none/all/name and the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: No demonstration of the multi-name syntax `anchor-scope: --a, --b`. No demo of inheritance (scope on an ancestor affecting descendants — distinct from scope on the host of the anchor).

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, no static-card, --accent-blue and --accent-emerald floaters both pair with --bg-ivory (passes AA).

openQuestions.title: Add the multi-name option to the dropdown
openQuestions.detail: Spec accepts `anchor-scope: --a, --b` to confine multiple names while letting others leak. Add a fourth option to demonstrate this — would distinguish the dashed-ident value from `all` clearly.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate ancestor inheritance
openQuestions.detail: anchor-scope can be set on an ancestor of the anchor host (not just the host). Add a wrapper with anchor-scope: all and watch all descendant anchors confined to that wrapper.

summary: Resolved the required ChromeStatus reference; 2 follow-up question(s) remain.
